source_summary: Household move coordination notes covering a 3–4 week window with lease overlap, budget limits, pet requirements, visa timing, furniture decisions, and unclear task ownership.
decision_context: The household must sequence move logistics under overlapping leases, building pet approval, uncertain visa timing, elevator constraints for movers, and divided ownership of utilities and address changes.
stated_decision: Decide move sequencing, ownership, and which commitments to make before building and visa answers are final.
implied_decision: Whether to accept overlap rent and storage costs for a mid-month move or delay to reduce overlap while risking visa and painter dependencies.
goals: Confirm a workable move date window
Meet building pet and lease requirements before keys
Stay within a $4–5k mover and deposit budget
Assign clear owners for utilities, address changes, and mover booking
options_considered: Move on the 14th with overlap rent and short-term storage for the couch
Delay move to the 22nd to reduce overlap but risk visa timing and painter slip
Hire movers for heavy items only and handle boxes DIY to save about $1.2k
Use parent help for boxes and boarding for the dog during move weekend
constraints: New lease starts the 12th; old lease ends the 30th
Budget of roughly $4–5k for movers and deposits
Building requires pet registration before keys
Freight elevator slot must be confirmed before mover booking
Both partners are working full time during packing window
risks: Visa delay could block lease signing in both names and utility setup
Painter slip could force storage costs or overlap rent extension
Elevator hoist surcharge could blow the mover budget
Unclear ownership could leave utilities or address changes undone
DIY packing could slip while working full time
assumptions: Pet paperwork can clear before move-in if submitted immediately
Movers will hold a cancellable slot once elevator time is confirmed
Parents can help with boxes but not heavy furniture
Boarding is only needed if the new gate is not ready move weekend
evidence: Movers quoted extra if hoist is required for the small freight elevator
Building stated keys depend on pet deposit and registration
Partner visa renewal appointment is the week before the preferred move
Overlap rent for 12th–30th is straining the budget
open_questions: Will pet approval clear before the target move date?
What is the visa outcome timeline relative to lease signing?
Which elevator slot is available for booking?
Who owns utilities, address changes, and mover coordination?
tensions: Mid-month move reduces painter risk but increases overlap rent
DIY packing saves money but conflicts with full-time work
Booking movers early reduces risk but depends on unresolved building answers
Selling the couch saves storage cost but removes fallback furniture
recommendation_candidate: Assign a single move owner, submit pet paperwork immediately, confirm elevator slot and mover quote, and target a mid-month move with explicit storage plan for the couch only if painter timing slips.
missing_context: Confirmed pet approval timeline from building management
Final mover quote with elevator constraints
suggested_next_steps: Name one owner for the move checklist today
Submit pet records and deposit to building management
Request elevator slot and locked mover quote with cancellation terms
Decide sell-vs-store for the couch once storage cost is known
Hold a 30-minute sequencing review after visa appointment
